page: Amazon Web Services c6i.large is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 1 physical core with 2 threads, and 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. This configuration yields 2.0 GB of memory per core. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1881.49 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1622.0. However, multi-core and memory-bound workloads show distinct limitations. The stress-ng multi-core performance is 1922.07, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 1856.0. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block bandwidth at 14.26 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 13.09 GB/sec.

The c6i.large offers a dedicated compute profile at an entry-level scale, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for single-threaded tasks that do not require large memory footprints. The 4.0 GB memory capacity and low multi-core scaling present clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive or highly parallel applications. It is best suited for lightweight compute workloads, single-threaded batch processing, basic web serving, and simple text processing tasks.

It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory, with no local storage or GPU acceleration. Single-core benchmarks demonstrate average performance, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low. This resource profile presents a tradeoff between dedicated CPU execution and limited memory capacity. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level compute tasks but less effective for memory-heavy or highly parallel workloads. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, single-threaded batch processing, and basic text processing.

tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance with Intel Xeon architecture, designed for lightweight single-threaded processing and basic web serving.
